subject: Cloud Computing
title: Carbon-Aware Computing
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Carbon-Aware Computing.

hook.text: What if your workload ran when electricity was cleanest? Carbon-aware computing schedules by grid emissions.

buildup.text: The carbon intensity of electricity varies by time and location. Midnight wind power is cleaner than noon coal power.

discovery.text: Google already shifts workloads to data centers with the cleanest energy. It's called carbon-intelligent computing.

twist.text: Most workloads can shift by minutes or hours without users noticing. Batch jobs, backups, and training can wait.

climax.text: The Green Software Foundation is standardizing carbon-aware APIs. Any developer can build carbon-aware apps soon.

punchline.text: Run your code when the grid is greenest. Timing matters.

quiz.question: How does carbon-aware computing reduce emissions?
options: By scheduling workloads to run when and where electricity has the lowest carbon intensity
By turning off all data centers during peak hours
By using only solar-powered devices for cloud computing
